messages.content: Based on the provided context, K-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) is a type of instance-based learning classifier. It does not require any training but instead classifies new data points based on the k nearest training examples in the feature space. The classification is done by a simple majority vote of the nearest neighbors. The time complexity for making predictions is O(n) since it needs to find the nearest neighbors. K-NN assumes that similar data points have similar outcomes.
